facts.text: Bridge L-158, a railroad bridge crossing the Muscoot Reservoir near Goldens Bridge, was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1978.
Goldens Bridge sits beside the Croton River and lies adjacent to Interstate 684, placing it in western Westchester County.
The hamlet’s name has evolved over time, appearing as Coldens Bridge, Goldings Bridge, and Goldens Bridge on early maps.
The original bridge was washed away by floods and rebuilt multiple times until the Croton Watershed dams controlled the river.
A local nonprofit, the Goldens Bridge Hamlet Organization, works with residents, businesses, and officials to preserve the hamlet’s historic and natural beauty.
Goldens Bridge is about 40 miles north of Midtown Manhattan, making it a northern suburb of New York City.
